title: Citric acid limescale remover
subtitle: A 10% citric acid spray for taps, showerheads, and kettle scale with tea tree essential oil
excerpt: Limescale is calcium carbonate, a base, dissolved readily by acids. A 10% citric acid solution applied to taps, showerheads, and kettle elements and left to dwell for 20 minutes removes moderate buildup without scrubbing. For stone surfaces, test a hidden area first.
sourceNotes: Citric acid limescale removal chemistry from standard household chemistry references.

recipe.batchNotes: Make in larger quantities and store in the spray bottle.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in the spray bottle. Citric acid is very stable in solution. Discard if cloudiness develops.

glossaryTerms.definition: Calcium carbonate (CaCO3) deposited from hard water when it is heated or evaporates. It builds up on taps, showerheads, kettle elements, and dishwasher surfaces. Being alkaline, it is dissolved by acids.
A weak organic acid naturally abundant in citrus fruit. It dissolves limescale by reacting with calcium carbonate to form calcium citrate, which is water-soluble and rinses away.

The period a cleaning product is left in contact with a surface before rinsing. For citric acid on limescale, a 20-minute dwell time is sufficient for light to moderate buildup; heavy scale may need a second application.

Natural stone surfaces including marble, travertine, limestone, and slate contain calcium carbonate and will etch on contact with acidic cleaners. Always test on a hidden area before applying acid-based cleaners to stone.

content.text: through a straightforward acid-base reaction. A 10% citric acid solution in distilled water is effective and safe for chrome, ceramic, glass, and plastic. A 20-minute
removes light to moderate buildup; heavy scale responds better to two applications. Always check the
caution below if your bathroom has marble, limestone, or slate surfaces. Shelf life is 6 months.
Dissolve citric acid powder in distilled water and stir until completely clear. Add tea tree essential oil and shake to distribute. Pour into the spray bottle.

To use: spray generously onto the limescale-affected surface. Leave for 20 minutes. For showerheads, fill a small plastic bag with the solution and secure around the showerhead for 20 to 30 minutes. Wipe clean with a damp cloth. Rinse with water. Repeat for stubborn scale.

Store in the spray bottle. Shelf life is 6 months. Citric acid solution is very stable. Do not use on natural stone (marble, limestone, travertine, slate) without testing on a hidden area first. Do not mix with bleach.
